 {"id":"bd-clua","title":"resolv: add host-parity ABI probes for synchronous-only async-DNS wrappers","description":"Capture concrete glibc behavior for getaddrinfo_a/gai_error/gai_cancel/gai_suspend degenerate cases in focused ABI probes so future resolver stub work is grounded in observed host semantics instead of assumptions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:27:54.963463136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:06:13.918812607Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:06:13.916727971Z","close_reason":"Added host-parity getaddrinfo_a mode matrix probes and aligned invalid-mode degenerate behavior with observed host libc semantics.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cmiv","title":"EPIC: Implement real DNS resolution for getaddrinfo","description":"getaddrinfo() currently only resolves:
1. Numeric IP addresses (127.0.0.1, ::1)
2. Hostnames found in /etc/hosts

It does NOT perform actual DNS queries. This means programs like wget, curl (for non-localhost URLs), dig, nslookup, and any network client that resolves hostnames will fail.

OPTIONS:
A) Delegate to host libc's getaddrinfo for non-numeric, non-/etc/hosts hostnames
B) Implement a minimal DNS stub resolver (send UDP query to nameserver from /etc/resolv.conf)
C) Both: try our resolver first, fall back to host

Option A is fastest to implement and most reliable. Option B gives us full independence from host libc.

ALREADY DONE: /etc/hosts lookup works, numeric IP parsing works, freeaddrinfo correctly frees our Box-allocated structures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:15.603558212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T18:43:31.403160236Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T18:43:31.402959135Z","close_reason":"Already implemented. getaddrinfo has a full native DNS stub resolver: udp_dns_query() sends A/AAAA queries to /etc/resolv.conf nameservers via SYS_socket/SYS_sendto/SYS_recvfrom with configurable timeout/retry. ResolverConfig parses nameservers, search domains, ndots. Resolution chain: numeric -> /etc/hosts -> DNS -> repair fallback. 46 resolv tests pass. Previously verified when closing bd-zh1y.7 and bd-1y7.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cmiv.1","title":"Add host libc delegation for DNS-requiring getaddrinfo calls","description":"When getaddrinfo receives a hostname that:
1. Is NOT a numeric IP address
2. Is NOT found in /etc/hosts

Delegate to the host libc's getaddrinfo via our ELF resolver. The host's getaddrinfo will perform the actual DNS query.

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. resolve_host_symbol_raw("getaddrinfo") to get host's function pointer
2. Call host_getaddrinfo(node, service, hints, res) 
3. Return the host's result directly (the addrinfo structs are heap-allocated by the host)
4. In freeaddrinfo, detect whether the addrinfo was allocated by us (Box) or host (malloc) and free accordingly

TRICKY PART: freeaddrinfo must distinguish our Box-allocated addrinfos from host-malloc-allocated ones. Could use a sentinel field or check if the pointer is in our Box allocation range.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:15.811880807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:30:57.450087855Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:30:57.448613314Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cmiv.1","depends_on_id":"bd-cmiv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:15.811880807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:39:25.750825423Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:39:25.750825423Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:39:25.750825423Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T01:18:55.383949478Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T01:18:55.381776786Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-co1f","title":"Conformance: add execute_* handlers for poll_ops family (2 symbols)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:33:37.479657348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:35:06.709712104Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T01:35:06.707626228Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-cs9w","title":"Left-Right Sync for complex metadata reads (section 14.3)","description":"Implement Left-Right synchronization for wait-free reads of complex metadata structures in FrankenLibC. Left-Right maintains two copies of the data structure (left and right). Readers always access one copy wait-free (no locks, no atomics on the read path). Writers update the inactive copy, then toggle the active pointer, then wait for readers to drain from the old copy before updating it too. Compared to RCU (bd-3aof), Left-Right provides: (a) Wait-free reads (not just lock-free). (b) Works for non-RCU-friendly data structures (those requiring consistent snapshots of multiple fields). Application to FrankenLibC: (1) Allocator configuration — the allocator's configuration (size-class table, arena count, thread-to-arena mapping) is a complex multi-field structure that must be read consistently. Left-Right provides atomic snapshot reads without locks. (2) TSM policy table — the TSM's decision policy (state machine transitions, threshold parameters) may be updated at runtime (e.g., when switching from strict to hardened mode). Left-Right ensures readers always see a consistent policy snapshot. (3) Symbol dispatch table — the IFUNC-like dispatch table mapping symbols to implementations. Updated when CPU features change (e.g., AVX-512 becomes available after cpuid check). Implementation: LeftRight<T> wrapper with read() -> &T (wait-free) and write(|t: &mut T| ...) (serialized, may block briefly).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.3 of graveyard. Left-Right from Ramalhete & Correia (2015). Wait-free reads with consistent snapshots for multi-field structures.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- LeftRight<T: Clone>: maintains two copies (left: Box<T>, right: Box<T>) with version_index: AtomicBool.
- read() -> ReadGuard<T>: returns reference to active copy. No atomic RMW on read path (single atomic load of version_index).
- ReadGuard registers in per-reader arrival/departure counters (2 counters, one per version).
- write(|t: &mut T| ...) -> (): acquires writer mutex, updates inactive copy, toggles version_index, waits for readers on old version to drain, updates other copy.
- Reader drain: spin on reader counter for old version until zero. Bounded wait if readers are well-behaved.
- T: Clone required to synchronize the two copies after toggle.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. LeftRight<T> compiles and passes miri for basic read/write cycle.
2. Read path is wait-free: no CAS, no RMW (verified by cargo asm inspection).
3. Consistent snapshot: multi-field read always sees consistent state (test with 2-field struct updated atomically).
4. 16-reader + 1-writer stress test: 1M operations, TSan clean.
5. Read latency <3ns at p99 (compare with RCU at <2ns and RwLock at ~15ns).
6. Writer latency <1us including reader drain wait at p99 under 16-reader load.
7. No starvation: writer completes within bounded time even with continuous readers.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- tracing::trace!(target: left_right, operation=read, version, reader_count) per read (disabled in release).
- tracing::debug!(target: left_right, operation=write, version_toggled, drain_wait_ns) per write.
- tracing::warn!(target: left_right, slow_drain, drain_wait_ns, remaining_readers) if drain exceeds 100us.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:32.674331698Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T18:34:45.507096513Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T18:34:45.506818727Z","close_reason":"Implemented LeftRight synchronization in frankenlibc-membrane with cached readers, drain coordination, diagnostics, and 11 focused tests. Verified with rch exec cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane left_right -- --nocapture, rch exec cargo check --workspace --all-targets, and rch exec cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-membrane --all-targets -- -D warnings. Full-workspace clippy/fmt remain blocked by unrelated pre-existing ABI-file issues in the dirty worktree.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cs9w","depends_on_id":"bd-3aof","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-cv9","title":"Kernel: Approachability controller (tests + perf)","description":"Validate guarantees and overhead.

Tests:
- Simulated regimes: ensure controller reacts and returns toward safe set.
- Determinism tests.

Perf:
- Bench decide/observe deltas.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleOwl","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:33:08.426558889Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:21:30.894651019Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T19:21:30.894628216Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cv9","depends_on_id":"bd-242","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-cv9","depends_on_id":"bd-276","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-cv9","depends_on_id":"bd-d5l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
